# Leizilla-Site Implementation Plan

> **Handoff Document**: Complete specification for implementing the leizilla-site repository
>
> **Date**: 2025-10-31
> **Engine Repository**: https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla
> **Target Site Repository**: https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla-site (to be created)

---

## 🎯 Project Overview

**leizilla-site** is a static web frontend for visualizing Brazilian law datasets generated by the **leizilla engine**.

### Key Architectural Decisions

1. **Separate repositories**: Engine and site are independent projects
2. **uvx execution model**: Site executes engine via `uvx git+https://...`
3. **Version pinning**: Site pins to stable engine versions, upgrades independently
4. **GitHub Actions as compute**: No servers, GitHub Actions runs the engine
5. **100% static hosting**: GitHub Pages hosts the frontend
6. **DuckDB-WASM**: Client-side SQL queries on Parquet datasets

---

## 📐 Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  leizilla (Engine Repo)                                     │
│  - Python CLI for crawling, ETL, exports                    │
│  - Versioned releases (v1.0.0, v1.1.0, etc.)               │
│  - Consumed via: uvx git+https://...leizilla.git@v1.2.3    │
└──────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────┘
                       │
                       │ GitHub Actions executes via uvx
                       ▼
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  leizilla-site (Frontend Repo)                              │
│  - Static HTML/CSS/JavaScript                               │
│  - GitHub Actions workflows (scheduled + manual)            │
│  - Triggers engine via uvx in Actions runners               │
│  - Exports datasets to GitHub Releases                      │
│  - Hosted on GitHub Pages                                   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                       │
                       │ Users access via browser
                       ▼
              https://leizilla.github.io
```

---

## 🗂️ Repository Structure

Create the following structure for **leizilla-site**:

```
leizilla-site/
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/
│       ├── scheduled-crawl.yml      # Weekly automated crawl
│       ├── manual-crawl.yml         # On-demand crawl (workflow_dispatch)
│       ├── deploy-site.yml          # Deploy to GitHub Pages
│       └── test-engine.yml          # Test new engine versions
│
├── public/
│   ├── index.html                   # Homepage
│   ├── browse.html                  # Law browser interface
│   ├── search.html                  # Search interface
│   ├── datasets.html                # Download datasets
│   ├── docs.html                    # Documentation
│   ├── admin.html                   # Admin panel (trigger crawls)
│   └── about.html                   # About the project
│
├── src/
│   ├── js/
│   │   ├── data-loader.js          # Load datasets from Releases
│   │   ├── duckdb-wrapper.js       # DuckDB-WASM abstraction
│   │   ├── search.js               # Search logic
│   │   ├── browser.js              # Browse/filter logic
│   │   ├── workflow-trigger.js     # GitHub API to trigger Actions
│   │   └── utils.js                # Common utilities
│   │
│   ├── css/
│   │   ├── main.css                # Global styles
│   │   ├── components.css          # Component styles
│   │   └── variables.css           # CSS variables
│   │
│   └── lib/
│       └── duckdb-wasm/            # DuckDB WASM binaries (vendored)
│
├── scripts/
│   ├── fetch-latest-data.js        # Download latest datasets
│   └── generate-index.js           # Generate dataset index
│
├── docs/
│   ├── ENGINE_VERSION.md           # Document engine version compatibility
│   ├── DEPLOYMENT.md               # Deployment instructions
│   └── API.md                      # Data schema documentation
│
├── tests/
│   └── search.test.js              # Basic JS tests
│
├── .gitignore
├── package.json
├── vite.config.js                  # Vite for bundling (optional)
├── README.md
└── CLAUDE.md                        # Instructions for Claude Code
```

---

## 🔧 GitHub Actions Workflows

### 1. Scheduled Crawl (`scheduled-crawl.yml`)

Runs the engine weekly to keep data fresh.

```yaml
name: Scheduled Data Crawl

on:
  schedule:
    # Every Sunday at 2 AM UTC
    - cron: '0 2 * * 0'
  workflow_dispatch:
    # Allow manual trigger from GitHub UI

env:
  # Pin to specific engine version for stability
  ENGINE_VERSION: v1.2.3
  ENGINE_REPO: franklinbaldo/leizilla

jobs:
  crawl-rondonia:
    name: Crawl Rondônia Laws
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout site repo (for artifacts)
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Python with uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v1

      - name: Run discovery
        run: |
          uvx git+https://github.com/${{ env.ENGINE_REPO }}.git@${{ env.ENGINE_VERSION }} \
            leizilla discover \
            --origem rondonia \
            --start-coddoc 1 \
            --end-coddoc 10000 \
            --crawler-type simple

      - name: Download PDFs
        run: |
          uvx git+https://github.com/${{ env.ENGINE_REPO }}.git@${{ env.ENGINE_VERSION }} \
            leizilla download \
            --origem rondonia \
            --limit 100
        env:
          # If needed for credentials
          IA_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.IA_ACCESS_KEY }}
          IA_SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.IA_SECRET_KEY }}

      - name: Upload to Internet Archive
        run: |
          uvx git+https://github.com/${{ env.ENGINE_REPO }}.git@${{ env.ENGINE_VERSION }} \
            leizilla upload \
            --limit 100
        env:
          IA_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.IA_ACCESS_KEY }}
          IA_SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.IA_SECRET_KEY }}

      - name: Export datasets
        run: |
          uvx git+https://github.com/${{ env.ENGINE_REPO }}.git@${{ env.ENGINE_VERSION }} \
            leizilla export \
            --origem rondonia \
            --format parquet

      - name: Generate dataset index
        run: node scripts/generate-index.js

      - name: Create GitHub Release with datasets
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
        with:
          tag_name: data-${{ github.run_number }}
          name: Dataset Release ${{ github.run_number }}
          body: |
            **Automated Dataset Release**

            - **Origin**: Rondônia
            - **Generated**: ${{ github.event.repository.updated_at }}
            - **Engine Version**: ${{ env.ENGINE_VERSION }}
            - **Laws Count**: See leizilla_index.json

            ## Files
            - `leis_rondonia_completo.parquet` - Complete Rondônia dataset
            - `leizilla_index.json` - Dataset metadata and statistics
          files: |
            data/exports/*.parquet
            data/exports/leizilla_index.json
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
```

---

### 2. Manual Crawl (`manual-crawl.yml`)

Allows on-demand crawling with custom parameters.

```yaml
name: Manual Crawl

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      origem:
        description: 'Origin to crawl (rondonia, acre, etc.)'
        required: true
        type: choice
        options:
          - rondonia
          - acre
          - amazonas
        default: 'rondonia'

      start_coddoc:
        description: 'Start document ID'
        required: true
        type: number
        default: 1

      end_coddoc:
        description: 'End document ID'
        required: true
        type: number
        default: 100

      limit:
        description: 'Download/upload limit'
        required: false
        type: number
        default: 10

      engine_version:
        description: 'Engine version to use (e.g., v1.2.3 or main)'
        required: false
        type: string
        default: 'v1.2.3'

jobs:
  manual-crawl:
    name: Manual Crawl - ${{ github.event.inputs.origem }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v1

      - name: Discover laws
        run: |
          uvx git+https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla.git@${{ github.event.inputs.engine_version }} \
            leizilla discover \
            --origem ${{ github.event.inputs.origem }} \
            --start-coddoc ${{ github.event.inputs.start_coddoc }} \
            --end-coddoc ${{ github.event.inputs.end_coddoc }} \
            --crawler-type simple

      - name: Download PDFs
        run: |
          uvx git+https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla.git@${{ github.event.inputs.engine_version }} \
            leizilla download \
            --origem ${{ github.event.inputs.origem }} \
            --limit ${{ github.event.inputs.limit }}

      - name: Upload to IA
        run: |
          uvx git+https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla.git@${{ github.event.inputs.engine_version }} \
            leizilla upload \
            --limit ${{ github.event.inputs.limit }}
        env:
          IA_ACCESS_KEY: ${{ secrets.IA_ACCESS_KEY }}
          IA_SECRET_KEY: ${{ secrets.IA_SECRET_KEY }}

      - name: Export dataset
        run: |
          uvx git+https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla.git@${{ github.event.inputs.engine_version }} \
            leizilla export \
            --origem ${{ github.event.inputs.origem }}

      - name: Upload results as artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: crawl-${{ github.event.inputs.origem }}-${{ github.run_number }}
          path: data/exports/
          retention-days: 30
```

---

### 3. Deploy Site (`deploy-site.yml`)

Builds and deploys the static site to GitHub Pages.

```yaml
name: Deploy Site

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]
  workflow_dispatch:

permissions:
  contents: read
  pages: write
  id-token: write

jobs:
  build-deploy:
    name: Build and Deploy
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Fetch latest dataset index
        run: node scripts/fetch-latest-data.js

      - name: Build site
        run: npm run build

      - name: Setup Pages
        uses: actions/configure-pages@v4

      - name: Upload artifact
        uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3
        with:
          path: './dist'

      - name: Deploy to GitHub Pages
        id: deployment
        uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4
```

---

### 4. Test Engine Version (`test-engine.yml`)

Test new engine versions before pinning them.

```yaml
name: Test Engine Version

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      engine_version:
        description: 'Engine version to test (e.g., v1.3.0, main)'
        required: true
        type: string

jobs:
  test-engine:
    name: Test Engine ${{ github.event.inputs.engine_version }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
      - name: Setup uv
        uses: astral-sh/setup-uv@v1

      - name: Test engine installation
        run: |
          uvx git+https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla.git@${{ github.event.inputs.engine_version }} \
            leizilla --help

      - name: Test discovery (small sample)
        run: |
          uvx git+https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla.git@${{ github.event.inputs.engine_version }} \
            leizilla discover \
            --origem rondonia \
            --start-coddoc 1 \
            --end-coddoc 5 \
            --crawler-type simple

      - name: Test export
        run: |
          uvx git+https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla.git@${{ github.event.inputs.engine_version }} \
            leizilla export \
            --origem rondonia

      - name: Verify output
        run: |
          ls -lah data/exports/

      - name: Comment on success
        run: |
          echo "✅ Engine version ${{ github.event.inputs.engine_version }} works!"
          echo "Update ENGINE_VERSION in scheduled-crawl.yml to pin this version."
```

---

## 🌐 Frontend Implementation

### Data Loader (`src/js/data-loader.js`)

```javascript
/**
 * Load dataset index from GitHub Releases
 */
export async function loadDatasetIndex() {
  const owner = 'franklinbaldo';
  const repo = 'leizilla-site';

  // Get latest release with data
  const response = await fetch(
    `https://api.github.com/repos/${owner}/${repo}/releases`
  );
  const releases = await response.json();

  // Find latest release with data- prefix
  const dataRelease = releases.find(r => r.tag_name.startsWith('data-'));

  if (!dataRelease) {
    throw new Error('No dataset releases found');
  }

  // Download leizilla_index.json from release
  const indexAsset = dataRelease.assets.find(a =>
    a.name === 'leizilla_index.json'
  );

  if (!indexAsset) {
    throw new Error('Dataset index not found in release');
  }

  const indexResponse = await fetch(indexAsset.browser_download_url);
  return await indexResponse.json();
}

/**
 * Load Parquet dataset into DuckDB-WASM
 */
export async function loadDatasetIntoDb(db, datasetUrl) {
  // Register remote Parquet file
  await db.registerFileURL('laws.parquet', datasetUrl);

  // Create view for easier querying
  await db.exec(`
    CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW leis AS
    SELECT * FROM 'laws.parquet'
  `);

  return db;
}

/**
 * Get dataset download URL from index
 */
export function getDatasetUrl(index, origem, year = null) {
  const dataset = index.datasets.find(d => {
    if (year) {
      return d.origem === origem && d.year === year;
    }
    return d.origem === origem && !d.year; // Complete dataset
  });

  if (!dataset) {
    throw new Error(`Dataset not found: ${origem} ${year || 'complete'}`);
  }

  return dataset.download_url;
}
```

---

### DuckDB Wrapper (`src/js/duckdb-wrapper.js`)

```javascript
import * as duckdb from '@duckdb/duckdb-wasm';

let dbInstance = null;

/**
 * Initialize DuckDB-WASM instance
 */
export async function initDuckDb() {
  if (dbInstance) return dbInstance;

  const JSDELIVR_BUNDLES = duckdb.getJsDelivrBundles();

  const bundle = await duckdb.selectBundle(JSDELIVR_BUNDLES);
  const worker_url = URL.createObjectURL(
    new Blob([`importScripts("${bundle.mainWorker}");`], {
      type: 'text/javascript'
    })
  );

  const worker = new Worker(worker_url);
  const logger = new duckdb.ConsoleLogger();
  const db = new duckdb.AsyncDuckDB(logger, worker);

  await db.instantiate(bundle.mainModule);

  const conn = await db.connect();

  dbInstance = { db, conn };
  return dbInstance;
}

/**
 * Execute SQL query
 */
export async function query(sql, params = []) {
  const { conn } = await initDuckDb();

  const result = await conn.query(sql);
  return result.toArray().map(row => row.toJSON());
}

/**
 * Register remote Parquet file
 */
export async function registerParquet(url, tableName = 'laws') {
  const { conn } = await initDuckDb();

  await conn.query(`
    CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW ${tableName} AS
    SELECT * FROM read_parquet('${url}')
  `);
}
```

---

### Search Interface (`src/js/search.js`)

```javascript
import { query } from './duckdb-wrapper.js';

/**
 * Search laws by text
 */
export async function searchLaws(searchText, filters = {}) {
  const { origem, year, tipo, limit = 100 } = filters;

  let sql = `
    SELECT
      id,
      titulo,
      origem,
      tipo_lei,
      numero,
      ano,
      url_pdf_ia,
      url_original
    FROM leis
    WHERE 1=1
  `;

  const params = [];

  // Text search
  if (searchText) {
    sql += ` AND (
      titulo ILIKE ?
      OR conteudo ILIKE ?
    )`;
    params.push(`%${searchText}%`, `%${searchText}%`);
  }

  // Filters
  if (origem) {
    sql += ` AND origem = ?`;
    params.push(origem);
  }

  if (year) {
    sql += ` AND ano = ?`;
    params.push(year);
  }

  if (tipo) {
    sql += ` AND tipo_lei = ?`;
    params.push(tipo);
  }

  sql += ` ORDER BY ano DESC, numero DESC LIMIT ?`;
  params.push(limit);

  return await query(sql, params);
}

/**
 * Get law by ID
 */
export async function getLawById(id) {
  const results = await query(
    'SELECT * FROM leis WHERE id = ?',
    [id]
  );

  return results[0] || null;
}

/**
 * Get statistics
 */
export async function getStats() {
  const stats = await query(`
    SELECT
      COUNT(*) as total,
      COUNT(DISTINCT origem) as origens,
      COUNT(DISTINCT ano) as years,
      MIN(ano) as earliest_year,
      MAX(ano) as latest_year
    FROM leis
  `);

  const byOrigem = await query(`
    SELECT origem, COUNT(*) as count
    FROM leis
    GROUP BY origem
    ORDER BY count DESC
  `);

  const byYear = await query(`
    SELECT ano, COUNT(*) as count
    FROM leis
    GROUP BY ano
    ORDER BY ano DESC
    LIMIT 20
  `);

  return {
    ...stats[0],
    byOrigem,
    byYear
  };
}
```

---

### Workflow Trigger (`src/js/workflow-trigger.js`)

```javascript
/**
 * Trigger manual crawl workflow
 */
export async function triggerCrawl(config) {
  const { origem, startCoddoc, endCoddoc, limit, engineVersion, githubToken } = config;

  const owner = 'franklinbaldo';
  const repo = 'leizilla-site';
  const workflowId = 'manual-crawl.yml';

  const response = await fetch(
    `https://api.github.com/repos/${owner}/${repo}/actions/workflows/${workflowId}/dispatches`,
    {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {
        'Accept': 'application/vnd.github+json',
        'Authorization': `Bearer ${githubToken}`,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json',
        'X-GitHub-Api-Version': '2022-11-28'
      },
      body: JSON.stringify({
        ref: 'main',
        inputs: {
          origem,
          start_coddoc: startCoddoc.toString(),
          end_coddoc: endCoddoc.toString(),
          limit: limit.toString(),
          engine_version: engineVersion || 'v1.2.3'
        }
      })
    }
  );

  if (response.status === 204) {
    return { success: true };
  } else {
    const error = await response.json();
    throw new Error(error.message || 'Failed to trigger workflow');
  }
}

/**
 * Get workflow runs
 */
export async function getWorkflowRuns(workflowId = 'manual-crawl.yml') {
  const owner = 'franklinbaldo';
  const repo = 'leizilla-site';

  const response = await fetch(
    `https://api.github.com/repos/${owner}/${repo}/actions/workflows/${workflowId}/runs?per_page=10`
  );

  const data = await response.json();

  return data.workflow_runs.map(run => ({
    id: run.id,
    status: run.status,
    conclusion: run.conclusion,
    created_at: run.created_at,
    updated_at: run.updated_at,
    html_url: run.html_url
  }));
}
```

---

## 🎨 HTML Pages

### Homepage (`public/index.html`)

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="pt-BR">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Leizilla - Leis Brasileiras em Dados Abertos</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/src/css/main.css">
</head>
<body>
  <header>
    <h1>🦖 Leizilla</h1>
    <p>O dinossauro que devora PDFs jurídicos e cospe dados abertos</p>

    <nav>
      <a href="/">Home</a>
      <a href="/browse.html">Navegar</a>
      <a href="/search.html">Buscar</a>
      <a href="/datasets.html">Datasets</a>
      <a href="/docs.html">Documentação</a>
      <a href="/about.html">Sobre</a>
    </nav>
  </header>

  <main>
    <section class="hero">
      <h2>Todas as leis brasileiras, 100% abertas</h2>
      <p>
        Acesse, busque e analise a legislação brasileira em formatos abertos.
        Dados processados, limpos e prontos para uso.
      </p>

      <div class="cta">
        <a href="/search.html" class="button primary">🔍 Buscar Leis</a>
        <a href="/datasets.html" class="button secondary">📦 Baixar Datasets</a>
      </div>
    </section>

    <section id="stats" class="stats">
      <h3>Estatísticas</h3>
      <div class="stats-grid">
        <div class="stat">
          <span class="stat-value" id="total-laws">—</span>
          <span class="stat-label">Leis indexadas</span>
        </div>
        <div class="stat">
          <span class="stat-value" id="total-origins">—</span>
          <span class="stat-label">Origens</span>
        </div>
        <div class="stat">
          <span class="stat-value" id="total-years">—</span>
          <span class="stat-label">Anos cobertos</span>
        </div>
      </div>
    </section>

    <section class="features">
      <h3>Recursos</h3>

      <div class="feature-grid">
        <div class="feature">
          <h4>🔍 Busca Avançada</h4>
          <p>Busca em texto completo usando SQL no navegador com DuckDB-WASM</p>
        </div>

        <div class="feature">
          <h4>📦 Datasets Abertos</h4>
          <p>Baixe tudo em Parquet, JSON Lines ou DuckDB para análise local</p>
        </div>

        <div class="feature">
          <h4>🌐 100% Estático</h4>
          <p>Sem servidores, sem backend. Tudo funciona no navegador.</p>
        </div>

        <div class="feature">
          <h4>🔓 Código Aberto</h4>
          <p>MIT License. Fork, modifique, contribua!</p>
        </div>
      </div>
    </section>
  </main>

  <footer>
    <p>
      Projeto <strong>Leizilla</strong> — Transparência radical em dados jurídicos
    </p>
    <p>
      <a href="https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla">GitHub: Engine</a> |
      <a href="https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla-site">GitHub: Site</a>
    </p>
  </footer>

  <script type="module">
    import { loadDatasetIndex } from '/src/js/data-loader.js';

    async function loadStats() {
      try {
        const index = await loadDatasetIndex();

        document.getElementById('total-laws').textContent =
          index.stats.total_laws.toLocaleString('pt-BR');

        document.getElementById('total-origins').textContent =
          index.stats.origins.length;

        document.getElementById('total-years').textContent =
          index.stats.years.length;

      } catch (error) {
        console.error('Failed to load stats:', error);
      }
    }

    loadStats();
  </script>
</body>
</html>
```

### Admin Panel (`public/admin.html`)

```html
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="pt-BR">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
  <title>Leizilla - Admin</title>
  <link rel="stylesheet" href="/src/css/main.css">
</head>
<body>
  <header>
    <h1>🦖 Leizilla Admin</h1>
    <nav>
      <a href="/">Home</a>
      <a href="/admin.html" class="active">Admin</a>
    </nav>
  </header>

  <main>
    <section class="admin-panel">
      <h2>Trigger Manual Crawl</h2>

      <div class="auth-section">
        <label>
          GitHub Token (needed to trigger workflows):
          <input type="password" id="github-token" placeholder="ghp_..." />
        </label>
        <p class="hint">
          Create a token at:
          <a href="https://github.com/settings/tokens" target="_blank">
            github.com/settings/tokens
          </a>
          <br>
          Required scope: <code>repo</code> or <code>workflow</code>
        </p>
      </div>

      <form id="crawl-form">
        <div class="form-group">
          <label>
            Origin:
            <select name="origem" required>
              <option value="rondonia">Rondônia</option>
              <option value="acre">Acre</option>
              <option value="amazonas">Amazonas</option>
            </select>
          </label>
        </div>

        <div class="form-row">
          <div class="form-group">
            <label>
              Start Coddoc:
              <input type="number" name="startCoddoc" value="1" required />
            </label>
          </div>

          <div class="form-group">
            <label>
              End Coddoc:
              <input type="number" name="endCoddoc" value="100" required />
            </label>
          </div>
        </div>

        <div class="form-group">
          <label>
            Limit (per step):
            <input type="number" name="limit" value="10" required />
          </label>
        </div>

        <div class="form-group">
          <label>
            Engine Version:
            <input type="text" name="engineVersion" value="v1.2.3" placeholder="v1.2.3 or main" />
          </label>
        </div>

        <button type="submit" class="button primary">🚀 Trigger Crawl</button>
      </form>

      <div id="status" class="status-message"></div>
    </section>

    <section class="workflow-runs">
      <h2>Recent Workflow Runs</h2>
      <div id="runs-list">Loading...</div>
    </section>
  </main>

  <script type="module">
    import { triggerCrawl, getWorkflowRuns } from '/src/js/workflow-trigger.js';

    // Load token from localStorage
    const tokenInput = document.getElementById('github-token');
    const savedToken = localStorage.getItem('github_token');
    if (savedToken) {
      tokenInput.value = savedToken;
    }

    // Save token on change
    tokenInput.addEventListener('change', () => {
      localStorage.setItem('github_token', tokenInput.value);
    });

    // Handle form submission
    document.getElementById('crawl-form').addEventListener('submit', async (e) => {
      e.preventDefault();

      const formData = new FormData(e.target);
      const githubToken = tokenInput.value;

      if (!githubToken) {
        alert('Please provide a GitHub token');
        return;
      }

      const statusDiv = document.getElementById('status');
      statusDiv.textContent = '⏳ Triggering workflow...';
      statusDiv.className = 'status-message pending';

      try {
        await triggerCrawl({
          origem: formData.get('origem'),
          startCoddoc: parseInt(formData.get('startCoddoc')),
          endCoddoc: parseInt(formData.get('endCoddoc')),
          limit: parseInt(formData.get('limit')),
          engineVersion: formData.get('engineVersion'),
          githubToken
        });

        statusDiv.textContent = '✅ Workflow triggered! Check GitHub Actions for progress.';
        statusDiv.className = 'status-message success';

        // Refresh runs list
        loadWorkflowRuns();

      } catch (error) {
        statusDiv.textContent = `❌ Error: ${error.message}`;
        statusDiv.className = 'status-message error';
      }
    });

    // Load recent workflow runs
    async function loadWorkflowRuns() {
      try {
        const runs = await getWorkflowRuns();

        const runsList = document.getElementById('runs-list');
        runsList.innerHTML = runs.map(run => `
          <div class="workflow-run ${run.status}">
            <div class="run-header">
              <span class="run-status ${run.conclusion || run.status}">
                ${getStatusIcon(run.status, run.conclusion)}
                ${run.conclusion || run.status}
              </span>
              <span class="run-date">
                ${new Date(run.created_at).toLocaleString('pt-BR')}
              </span>
            </div>
            <a href="${run.html_url}" target="_blank">
              View on GitHub →
            </a>
          </div>
        `).join('');

      } catch (error) {
        console.error('Failed to load workflow runs:', error);
      }
    }

    function getStatusIcon(status, conclusion) {
      if (status === 'completed') {
        return conclusion === 'success' ? '✅' : '❌';
      } else if (status === 'in_progress') {
        return '⏳';
      } else {
        return '⏸️';
      }
    }

    // Initial load
    loadWorkflowRuns();

    // Refresh every 30 seconds if there are in-progress runs
    setInterval(loadWorkflowRuns, 30000);
  </script>
</body>
</html>
```

---

## 📦 Package Configuration

### `package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "leizilla-site",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "Frontend for Leizilla - Brazilian law datasets visualization",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "vite",
    "build": "vite build",
    "preview": "vite preview",
    "fetch-data": "node scripts/fetch-latest-data.js",
    "generate-index": "node scripts/generate-index.js"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@duckdb/duckdb-wasm": "^1.28.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "vite": "^5.0.0"
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla-site.git"
  },
  "license": "MIT"
}
```

### `vite.config.js`

```javascript
import { defineConfig } from 'vite';

export default defineConfig({
  build: {
    outDir: 'dist',
    assetsDir: 'assets',
    rollupOptions: {
      input: {
        main: 'public/index.html',
        browse: 'public/browse.html',
        search: 'public/search.html',
        datasets: 'public/datasets.html',
        docs: 'public/docs.html',
        admin: 'public/admin.html',
        about: 'public/about.html'
      }
    }
  },
  server: {
    port: 3000
  }
});
```

## 📚 Documentation Files

### `docs/ENGINE_VERSION.md`

```markdown
# Engine Version Management

## Current Pinned Version

**Version**: `v1.2.3`
**Pinned Date**: 2025-10-31
**Compatible**: ✅

## Version History

| Version | Date       | Status | Notes                          |
|---------|------------|--------|--------------------------------|
| v1.2.3  | 2025-10-31 | ✅ Current | Stable, all features working |
| v1.2.2  | 2025-10-20 | ⚠️ Deprecated | Use v1.2.3 instead        |
| v1.2.1  | 2025-10-15 | ❌ Broken | Export bug fixed in v1.2.2 |

## Upgrading Engine Version

### Step 1: Test New Version

Run the test workflow:

```bash
# Via GitHub UI:
Actions → Test Engine Version → Run workflow
  engine_version: v1.3.0
```

### Step 2: Update Workflows

If tests pass, update these files:

1. `.github/workflows/scheduled-crawl.yml`
   ```yaml
   env:
     ENGINE_VERSION: v1.3.0  # <-- Update this
   ```

2. `docs/ENGINE_VERSION.md` (this file)
   - Update "Current Pinned Version"
   - Add entry to version history

### Step 3: Verify in Production

Monitor the next scheduled crawl to ensure it completes successfully.

## Rollback Procedure

If a new version causes issues:

1. Revert `ENGINE_VERSION` in workflows
2. Re-run the scheduled crawl
3. Document the issue in version history
```

---

## 🔐 Secrets Configuration

Add these secrets to the **leizilla-site** repository:

1. **`IA_ACCESS_KEY`** - Internet Archive access key (for uploads)
2. **`IA_SECRET_KEY`** - Internet Archive secret key (for uploads)

Go to: `Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions → New repository secret`

---

## 🚀 Deployment Instructions

### Initial Setup

1. **Create repository**:
   ```bash
   gh repo create franklinbaldo/leizilla-site --public
   ```

2. **Enable GitHub Pages**:
   - Go to: `Settings → Pages`
   - Source: `GitHub Actions`

3. **Add secrets** (IA credentials)

4. **Initialize project**:
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla-site.git
   cd leizilla-site
   npm install
   ```

5. **Test locally**:
   ```bash
   npm run dev
   # Visit http://localhost:3000
   ```

6. **Deploy**:
   ```bash
   git add .
   git commit -m "Initial commit"
   git push origin main
   # GitHub Actions will auto-deploy to Pages
   ```

---

### Manual Testing Checklist

Before merging to main:

- [ ] Test engine version upgrade workflow
- [ ] Trigger manual crawl (small sample)
- [ ] Verify dataset appears in Releases
- [ ] Load dataset in frontend search
- [ ] Test DuckDB-WASM queries
- [ ] Verify admin panel authentication
- [ ] Check responsive design on mobile

### Automated Tests (Future)

```bash
npm test  # Run unit tests for JS modules
```

## 📖 Data Schema Documentation

Document the Parquet schema in `docs/API.md`:

```markdown
# Data Schema

## Parquet Schema

File: `leis_{origem}_{year}.parquet`

| Column           | Type      | Description                        |
|------------------|-----------|------------------------------------|
| id               | INTEGER   | Unique identifier                  |
| titulo           | STRING    | Law title                          |
| origem           | STRING    | Origin (rondonia, acre, etc.)      |
| tipo_lei         | STRING    | Law type (lei, decreto, etc.)      |
| numero           | STRING    | Law number                         |
| ano              | INTEGER   | Year of publication                |
| data_publicacao  | DATE      | Publication date                   |
| url_original     | STRING    | Original government URL            |
| url_pdf_ia       | STRING    | Internet Archive PDF URL           |
| url_ocr_ia       | STRING    | Internet Archive OCR text URL      |
| conteudo         | STRING    | Full text content (OCR extracted)  |
| metadados        | STRING    | JSON metadata                      |
| created_at       | TIMESTAMP | Record creation timestamp          |
| updated_at       | TIMESTAMP | Last update timestamp              |

## Query Examples

### Search by text
```sql
SELECT titulo, ano, url_pdf_ia
FROM 'leis_rondonia_completo.parquet'
WHERE conteudo LIKE '%meio ambiente%'
ORDER BY ano DESC
LIMIT 10;
```

### Count by year
```sql
SELECT ano, COUNT(*) as total
FROM 'leis_rondonia_completo.parquet'
GROUP BY ano
ORDER BY ano DESC;
```
```

## 🔄 Versioning Strategy

### Engine Versioning (leizilla repo)

Uses semantic versioning: `v{major}.{minor}.{patch}`

- **Major**: Breaking changes to CLI interface or data schema
- **Minor**: New features, backward compatible
- **Patch**: Bug fixes

### Site Versioning (leizilla-site repo)

Independent versioning, also semantic:

- Pin to compatible engine versions in workflows
- Can use multiple engine versions for different crawl jobs

### Example Compatibility Matrix

| Site Version | Compatible Engine Versions |
|--------------|----------------------------|
| v1.0.0       | v1.2.x, v1.3.x             |
| v1.1.0       | v1.3.x, v1.4.x             |
| v2.0.0       | v2.0.x+                    |

---

## 📝 CLAUDE.md for leizilla-site

Create this file in the new repository:

```markdown
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code when working with the leizilla-site repository.

## Project Overview

**leizilla-site** is the static web frontend for visualizing Brazilian law datasets generated by the leizilla engine. It uses GitHub Actions to execute the engine via `uvx`, eliminating the need for traditional backend infrastructure.

## Architecture

- **Frontend**: Static HTML/CSS/JavaScript with DuckDB-WASM
- **Compute**: GitHub Actions executes leizilla engine via uvx
- **Data**: Parquet files from GitHub Releases
- **Hosting**: GitHub Pages (100% static)

## Engine Integration

The site executes the leizilla engine using uvx:

```bash
uvx git+https://github.com/franklinbaldo/leizilla.git@v1.2.3 leizilla <command>
```

**Current pinned version**: See `docs/ENGINE_VERSION.md`

## Development Commands

```bash
npm install          # Install dependencies
npm run dev          # Start dev server (http://localhost:3000)
npm run build        # Build for production
npm run preview      # Preview production build
npm run fetch-data   # Download latest datasets
```

## GitHub Actions Workflows

- `scheduled-crawl.yml` - Weekly automated data refresh
- `manual-crawl.yml` - On-demand crawl (workflow_dispatch)
- `deploy-site.yml` - Deploy to GitHub Pages
- `test-engine.yml` - Test new engine versions

## Key Files

- `.github/workflows/*.yml` - GitHub Actions workflows
- `public/*.html` - HTML pages
- `src/js/*.js` - JavaScript modules
- `docs/ENGINE_VERSION.md` - Engine version compatibility

## Upgrading Engine Version

1. Test new version: Actions → Test Engine Version
2. Update `ENGINE_VERSION` in `scheduled-crawl.yml`
3. Update `docs/ENGINE_VERSION.md`
4. Monitor next scheduled run

## Quality Standards

- ES Modules (type: "module")
- Vanilla JS or minimal framework
- Responsive design
- Accessibility (ARIA, semantic HTML)
- 100% static (no server-side code)
```

---

## ✅ Success Criteria

The leizilla-site implementation is complete when:

1. ✅ Repository created with all workflows
2. ✅ Scheduled crawl runs weekly via GitHub Actions
3. ✅ Manual crawl can be triggered from GitHub UI
4. ✅ Datasets exported to GitHub Releases
5. ✅ Frontend loads and displays data via DuckDB-WASM
6. ✅ Search functionality works
7. ✅ Site deployed to GitHub Pages
8. ✅ Admin panel can trigger workflows (authenticated)
9. ✅ Documentation complete (ENGINE_VERSION, API, README)
10. ✅ Engine version can be upgraded independently

---

## 🎓 Handoff Notes

### For the Next Claude Session

When implementing this plan:

1. **Start with workflows** - They're the backbone of the architecture
2. **Test uvx locally first** - Ensure engine executes correctly
3. **Use simple HTML initially** - Get data flowing before polishing UI
4. **DuckDB-WASM is key** - Spend time getting this right
5. **Pin engine version** - Don't use `@main` in production workflows

### Critical Dependencies

- **leizilla engine** must be functional and published
- **GitHub Actions** must have IA credentials (secrets)
- **DuckDB-WASM** library must be vendored or loaded from CDN
- **GitHub Pages** must be enabled in repository settings

### Common Pitfalls to Avoid

1. ❌ Don't hardcode file paths - use GitHub API to discover releases
2. ❌ Don't load huge Parquet files without streaming
3. ❌ Don't expose GitHub tokens in client-side code
4. ❌ Don't skip engine version testing before pinning
5. ❌ Don't forget CORS headers when loading remote files

---
